skill.name: ai-marketing-videos
skill.description: Create AI marketing videos for ads, promos, product launches, and brand content. Models: Veo, Seedance, Wan, FLUX for visuals, Kokoro for voiceover. Types: product demos, testimonials, explainers, social ads, brand videos. Use for: Facebook ads, YouTube ads, product launches, brand awareness. Triggers: marketing video, ad video, promo video, commercial, brand video, product video, explainer video, ad creative, video ad, facebook ad video, youtube ad, instagram ad, tiktok ad, promotional video, launch video
skill.summary: Create professional AI marketing videos for ads, social media, and brand content using Veo, Seedance, and Kokoro models
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell command patterns and hardcoded URLs, but evaluation confirms these are documentation examples only. The file contains markdown documentation with example bash commands for users to run. No malicious code, no prompt injection, no actual command execution. The 'pipe to shell' pattern is a standard CLI installation method used by many legitimate services.

medium_findings.title: Standard CLI Installation Pattern
medium_findings.description: The skill documents using 'curl | sh' to install the inference.sh CLI. This is a common installation pattern used by many legitimate tools including Docker. While not ideal from a security perspective, it is widely accepted and the URL points to the official service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a standard installation method used by many legitimate services. The risk is minimal as it's documented usage of the skill's own CLI tool.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documentation includes URLs to inference.sh services. These are legitimate service endpoints required for the skill to function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to official inference.sh service endpoints which are required for the skill to function.

low_findings.title: Example Bash Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'external_commands' patterns, but these are example bash commands in markdown documentation, not actual code execution. Users copy and run these commands themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are bash code blocks within a markdown file - documentation examples, not executable code. The skill does not execute these commands automatically.

content.value_statement: Generate professional marketing videos for ads, social media, and brand content using AI models like Veo, Seedance, and Kokoro for voiceovers.
actual_capabilities: Generate product promo videos using Google Veo and ByteDance Seedance models
Create voiceovers using Kokoro TTS with multiple voice options
Merge video clips with audio tracks using the media-merger tool
Produce platform-specific ads for Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, LinkedIn, and TikTok
Generate AI music for video soundtracks
limitations: Requires an inference.sh account and API access
Video generation credits depend on subscription tier
Output quality depends on prompt specificity
Some features require specific aspect ratios or duration limits

use_cases.title: Social Media Ad Campaigns
use_cases.description: Quickly generate multiple video ad variants for A/B testing across Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok with different hooks and messaging.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and social media managers

use_cases.title: Product Launch Videos
use_cases.description: Create professional product reveal videos with cinematic quality for launches, including voiceover and music.

use_cases.title: Brand Content Creation
use_cases.description: Produce brand story videos, testimonials, and feature highlights for website and advertising use.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and brand marketing professionals

prompt_templates.title: Simple Product Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product promo video showing a smartphone emerging from light particles with premium tech aesthetic and commercial quality lighting.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick product showcase

prompt_templates.title: Platform-Specific Ad
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 15-second vertical 9:16 Instagram ad with fast-paced product showcase, Gen-Z aesthetic, and hook in the first 2 seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Ad Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product ad with: 1) attention-grabbing hook in dramatic lighting, 2) relatable problem statement, 3) product solution reveal, 4) benefits showcase with lifestyle integration, 5) professional CTA with hero shot.
prompt_templates.scenario: When producing a full multi-scene advertisement

prompt_templates.title: Explainer Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Write and produce a 60-second explainer video script with hook, problem, solution, 3 key features, social proof, and CTA. Generate separate visual scenes for each section with professional voiceover.

output_examples.input: Generate a product launch video for a new fitness app
output_examples.output: A 30-second cinematic video showing a smartphone displaying the fitness app, with dynamic transitions between workout场景, happy users exercising, and the app interface. Professional voiceover says: 'Transform your fitness journey. Track your progress. Achieve your goals. Download now.'

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second TikTok ad for a coffee brand
output_examples.output: Vertical 9:16 video with quick cuts of coffee being poured, steam rising, satisfied customers drinking coffee in a trendy cafe. Upbeat music. Hook: close-up of coffee cup with '☕' text animation.

best_practices: Write specific, detailed prompts that describe visual style, mood, and key elements
Always include a strong hook in the first 3 seconds for ad effectiveness
Generate multiple variants with different hooks for A/B testing
Ensure videos work without sound by including text overlays
anti_patterns: Using vague prompts like 'make a cool video' - be specific about visuals and mood
Forgetting platform aspect ratios - always match 1:1, 9:16, or 16:9 to your target
Not including a clear call-to-action in the final seconds of ads
Generating long videos without clear structure - break into scenes for better results

faq.question: What AI models does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports Google Veo (2, 3, 3.1-fast), ByteDance Seedance, Wan, FLUX for visuals, and Kokoro for TTS voiceovers.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to sign up at inference.sh and obtain API credentials to use the video generation features.

faq.question: Can I use generated videos for commercial purposes?
faq.answer: Yes, videos generated through inference.sh are typically usable for commercial purposes. Check their terms of service for specific licensing details.

faq.question: How do I create videos for different platforms?
faq.answer: Use the platform-specific templates in the skill - 9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 1:1 for Instagram Feed, 16:9 for YouTube pre-roll.

faq.question: Can I add my own voiceover?
faq.answer: Yes, you can generate voiceovers using Kokoro TTS with various voice options, or upload your own audio files.

faq.question: How do I merge multiple video clips?
faq.answer: Use the infsh/media-merger tool to combine video clips, add audio tracks, and apply transitions between scenes.
